code_before:
---
# Apply side effects when specific config keys are changed.
# Input: _config_key (str), _config_value (str), instance_path

- name: Validate port range for HTTP_PORT
  ansible.builtin.fail:
    msg: "{{ _config_key }} must be between 1 and 65535 (got '{{ _config_value }}')"
  when:
    - _config_key in ['HTTP_PORT', 'HTTPS_PORT']
    - _config_value | int < 1 or _config_value | int > 65535

- name: Apply HTTPS_PORT side effects
  when: _config_key == 'HTTPS_PORT'
  block:
    - name: Read wikis.yaml for port update
      canasta_wikis_yaml:
        instance_path: "{{ instance_path }}"
        state: read
      register: _se_wikis

    - name: Compute new domain suffix
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        _port_suffix: "{{ (_config_value == '443') | ternary('', ':' + _config_value) }}"

    - name: Update MW_SITE_SERVER with new port
      canasta_env:
        path: "{{ instance_path }}/.env"
        state: set
        key: MW_SITE_SERVER
        value: "https://{{ _se_wikis.wikis[0].url | regex_replace('/.*$', '') | regex_replace(':[0-9]+$', '') }}{{ _port_suffix }}"
      when: _se_wikis.wikis | length > 0

    - name: Update MW_SITE_FQDN with new port
      canasta_env:
        path: "{{ instance_path }}/.env"
        state: set
        key: MW_SITE_FQDN
        value: "{{ _se_wikis.wikis[0].url | regex_replace('/.*$', '') | regex_replace(':[0-9]+$', '') }}{{ _port_suffix }}"
      when: _se_wikis.wikis | length > 0

- name: Apply CANASTA_ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY side effects
  when: _config_key == 'CANASTA_ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY' and _config_value | lower == 'true'
  ansible.builtin.include_role:
    name: orchestrator
    tasks_from: ensure_observability_credentials.yml
code_after:
---
# Apply side effects when specific config keys are changed.
# Input: _config_key (str), _config_value (str), instance_path

- name: Validate port is numeric
  ansible.builtin.fail:
    msg: "{{ _config_key }} must be a number (got '{{ _config_value }}')"
  when:
    - _config_key in ['HTTP_PORT', 'HTTPS_PORT']
    - _config_value | regex_search('^[0-9]+$') is none

- name: Validate port range for HTTP_PORT/HTTPS_PORT
  ansible.builtin.fail:
    msg: "{{ _config_key }} must be between 1 and 65535 (got '{{ _config_value }}')"
  when:
    - _config_key in ['HTTP_PORT', 'HTTPS_PORT']
    - _config_value | regex_search('^[0-9]+$') is not none
    - _config_value | int < 1 or _config_value | int > 65535

- name: Apply HTTPS_PORT side effects
  when: _config_key == 'HTTPS_PORT'
  block:
    - name: Read wikis.yaml for port update
      canasta_wikis_yaml:
        instance_path: "{{ instance_path }}"
        state: read
      register: _se_wikis

    - name: Extract domain (strip path and existing port)
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        _base_domain: "{{ _se_wikis.wikis[0].url | regex_replace('/.*$', '') | regex_replace(':[0-9]+$', '') }}"
      when: _se_wikis.wikis | length > 0

    - name: Compute new domain suffix
      ansible.builtin.set_fact:
        _port_suffix: "{{ (_config_value == '443') | ternary('', ':' + _config_value) }}"
      when: _se_wikis.wikis | length > 0

    - name: Update MW_SITE_SERVER with new port
      canasta_env:
        path: "{{ instance_path }}/.env"
        state: set
        key: MW_SITE_SERVER
        value: "https://{{ _base_domain }}{{ _port_suffix }}"
      when: _se_wikis.wikis | length > 0

    - name: Update MW_SITE_FQDN with new port (domain only, no scheme)
      canasta_env:
        path: "{{ instance_path }}/.env"
        state: set
        key: MW_SITE_FQDN
        value: "{{ _base_domain }}{{ _port_suffix }}"
      when: _se_wikis.wikis | length > 0

- name: Apply CANASTA_ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY side effects
  when: _config_key == 'CANASTA_ENABLE_OBSERVABILITY' and _config_value | lower == 'true'
  ansible.builtin.include_role:
    name: orchestrator
    tasks_from: ensure_observability_credentials.yml

code_before:
---
# tasks to setup a user on a host.
#
# Creates the user and adds the public ssh keys to the user.

# inputs:
#   - base_user_default: default user to setup
#   - base_user_password_prehashed: default user password prehashed
#   - base_user_additional_groups: additional user groups to be added to
#     base_user_add_ssh_keys: boolean to add ssh keys to the user


# block for base user tasks
- name: Block for base user tasks
  tags:
    - base
    - base.user
  block:
    # create the default user
    - name: Create the default user
      ansible.builtin.user:
        name: "{{ base_user_default }}"
        password: "{{ base_user_password_prehashed }}"
        update_password: on_create
        createhome: true
        home: "/home/{{ base_user_default }}"
        groups: "{{ base_user_groups + (base_user_additional_groups | default([])) }}"
        append: true
        state: present
        generate_ssh_key: true
        ssh_key_type: ed25519
      become: true

    # Add the public ssh keys to the user
    - name: Add the public ssh keys to the user
      ansible.posix.authorized_key:
        user: "{{ base_user_default }}"
        state: present
        key: "{{ item.key }}"
        comment: "{{ item.name }}"
      loop: "{{ secret_ssh_keys }}"
      loop_control:
        label: "{{ item.name }}"
      when: base_user_add_ssh_keys is true
code_after:
---
# tasks to setup a user on a host.
#
# Creates the user and adds the public ssh keys to the user.

# inputs:
#   - base_user_default: default user to setup
#   - base_user_password_prehashed: default user password prehashed
#   - base_user_additional_groups: additional user groups to be added to
#     base_user_add_ssh_keys: boolean to add ssh keys to the user


# block for base user tasks
- name: Block for base user tasks
  tags:
    - base
    - base.user
  block:
    # create the default user
    - name: Create the default user
      ansible.builtin.user:
        name: "{{ base_user_default }}"
        password: "{{ base_user_password_prehashed }}"
        update_password: on_create
        createhome: true
        home: "/home/{{ base_user_default }}"
        shell: "{{ base_user_shell }}"
        groups: "{{ base_user_groups + (base_user_additional_groups | default([])) }}"
        append: true
        state: present
        generate_ssh_key: true
        ssh_key_type: ed25519
      become: true

    # Add the public ssh keys to the user
    - name: Add the public ssh keys to the user
      ansible.posix.authorized_key:
        user: "{{ base_user_default }}"
        state: present
        key: "{{ item.key }}"
        comment: "{{ item.name }}"
      loop: "{{ secret_ssh_keys }}"
      loop_control:
        label: "{{ item.name }}"
      when: base_user_add_ssh_keys is true

code_before:
---
- name: Update all homelab nodes
  hosts: dns_servers:microcloud
  become: true
  serial: 1
  tags: [update]
  tasks:
    - name: Update apt cache and upgrade all packages
      ansible.builtin.apt:
        update_cache: true
        upgrade: full
        autoremove: true
        autoclean: true

    - name: Check if reboot is required
      ansible.builtin.stat:
        path: /var/run/reboot-required
      register: reboot_required
      changed_when: false

    # MicroCloud only: evacuate LXD instances before reboot
    - name: Evacuate LXD cluster member before reboot
      ansible.builtin.command: lxc cluster evacuate {{ inventory_hostname }} --force
      changed_when: true
      when:
        - inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']
        - reboot_required.stat.exists
      tags: [update, reboot]

    - name: Reboot if kernel was updated
      ansible.builtin.reboot:
        msg: "Reboot initiated by Ansible after package upgrade"
        connect_timeout: 5
        reboot_timeout: 600
        pre_reboot_delay: 0
        post_reboot_delay: 30
        test_command: whoami
      when: reboot_required.stat.exists
      tags: [update, reboot]

    - name: Verify Docker daemon is running
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: docker
        state: started

    # dns_servers: verify Technitium and Traefik are back

    - name: Verify Technitium is responding
      ansible.builtin.uri:
        url: "http://localhost:5380"
        status_code: 200
      register: technitium_check
      until: technitium_check.status == 200
      retries: 12
      delay: 10
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['dns_servers']

    - name: Verify Traefik API is responding
      ansible.builtin.uri:
        url: "http://localhost:8080/api/rawdata"
        status_code: 200
      register: traefik_check
      until: traefik_check.status == 200
      retries: 12
      delay: 10
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['dns_servers']

    # microcloud: verify LXD daemon + NFS, then restore cluster member
    - name: Verify LXD snap daemon is running
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: snap.lxd.daemon
        state: started
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']

    - name: Verify NFS datastore mount is active
      ansible.builtin.command: mountpoint /srv/datastore
      changed_when: false
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']

    - name: Restore LXD cluster member after reboot
      ansible.builtin.command: lxc cluster restore {{ inventory_hostname }}
      changed_when: true
      when:
        - inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']
        - reboot_required.stat.exists
      tags: [update, reboot]
code_after:
---
- name: Update all homelab nodes
  hosts: dns_servers:microcloud
  become: true
  serial: 1
  tags: [update]
  tasks:
    - name: Update apt cache and upgrade all packages
      ansible.builtin.apt:
        update_cache: true
        upgrade: full
        autoremove: true
        autoclean: true

    - name: Check if reboot is required
      ansible.builtin.stat:
        path: /var/run/reboot-required
      register: reboot_required
      changed_when: false

    # MicroCloud only: evacuate LXD instances before reboot
    - name: Evacuate LXD cluster member before reboot
      ansible.builtin.command: lxc cluster evacuate {{ mc_hostname }} --force
      changed_when: true
      when:
        - inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']
        - reboot_required.stat.exists
      tags: [update, reboot]

    - name: Reboot if kernel was updated
      ansible.builtin.reboot:
        msg: "Reboot initiated by Ansible after package upgrade"
        connect_timeout: 5
        reboot_timeout: 600
        pre_reboot_delay: 0
        post_reboot_delay: 30
        test_command: whoami
      when: reboot_required.stat.exists
      tags: [update, reboot]

    - name: Verify Docker daemon is running
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: docker
        state: started
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['dns_servers']

    # dns_servers: verify Technitium and Traefik are back

    - name: Verify Technitium is responding
      ansible.builtin.uri:
        url: "http://localhost:5380"
        status_code: 200
      register: technitium_check
      until: technitium_check.status == 200
      retries: 12
      delay: 10
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['dns_servers']

    - name: Verify Traefik API is responding
      ansible.builtin.uri:
        url: "http://localhost:8080/api/rawdata"
        status_code: 200
      register: traefik_check
      until: traefik_check.status == 200
      retries: 12
      delay: 10
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['dns_servers']

    # microcloud: verify LXD daemon + NFS, then restore cluster member
    - name: Verify LXD snap daemon is running
      ansible.builtin.systemd:
        name: snap.lxd.daemon
        state: started
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']

    - name: Verify NFS datastore mount is active
      ansible.builtin.command: mountpoint /srv/datastore
      changed_when: false
      when: inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']

    - name: Restore LXD cluster member after reboot
      ansible.builtin.command: lxc cluster restore {{ mc_hostname }}
      changed_when: true
      when:
        - inventory_hostname in groups['microcloud']
        - reboot_required.stat.exists
      tags: [update, reboot]

---
- name: Get list of all repositories
  uri:
    url: "{{ nexus_url }}/service/rest/v1/repositories"
    method: GET
    user: "{{ nexus_user }}"
    password: "{{ nexus_password }}"
    force_basic_auth: yes
    validate_certs: "{{ ssl_validate }}"
    return_content: yes
  register: all_repos
  changed_when: false
  tags: [configure, clean]

- name: Delete specified repositories
  uri:
    url: "{{ nexus_url }}/service/rest/v1/repositories/{{ item }}"
    method: DELETE
    user: "{{ nexus_user }}"
    password: "{{ nexus_password }}"
    force_basic_auth: yes
    validate_certs: "{{ ssl_validate }}"
    status_code: 204,404
  loop: "{{ nexus_repositories_to_delete | default([]) }}"
  vars:
    existing_names: "{{ all_repos.json | map(attribute='name') | list | default([]) }}"
  when: item in existing_names
  changed_when: false
  tags: [configure, clean]
code_after:
---
- name: Get list of all repositories
  ansible.builtin.uri:
    url: "{{ nexus_url }}/service/rest/v1/repositories"
    method: GET
    user: "{{ nexus_user }}"
    password: "{{ nexus_password }}"
    force_basic_auth: true
    validate_certs: "{{ ssl_validate }}"
    return_content: true
  register: all_repos
  no_log: true
  changed_when: false
  tags: [configure, clean]

- name: Delete specified repositories
  ansible.builtin.uri:
    url: "{{ nexus_url }}/service/rest/v1/repositories/{{ item }}"
    method: DELETE
    user: "{{ nexus_user }}"
    password: "{{ nexus_password }}"
    force_basic_auth: true
    validate_certs: "{{ ssl_validate }}"
    status_code: [204, 404]
  loop: "{{ nexus_repositories_to_delete | default([]) }}"
  vars:
    existing_names: "{{ all_repos.json | map(attribute='name') | list | default([]) }}"
  when: item in existing_names
  register: delete_result
  no_log: true
  changed_when: delete_result.status == 204
  tags: [configure, clean]

---
# Check Vault-Consul integration status
# Usage:
# uv run ansible-playbook playbooks/infrastructure/vault/vault-consul-status.yml -i inventory/environments/doggos-homelab/proxmox.yml

- name: Check Vault-Consul Integration Status
  hosts: nomad-server-1-lloyd
  gather_facts: false

  vars:
    consul_token: >-
      {{ lookup('infisical.vault.read_secrets',
                universal_auth_client_id=lookup('env', 'INFISICAL_UNIVERSAL_AUTH_CLIENT_ID'),
                universal_auth_client_secret=lookup('env', 'INFISICAL_UNIVERSAL_AUTH_CLIENT_SECRET'),
                project_id='7b832220-24c0-45bc-a5f1-ce9794a31259',
                env_slug='prod',
                path='/apollo-13/consul',
                secret_name='CONSUL_MASTER_TOKEN').value }}

  tasks:
    - name: Verify master token is set
      ansible.builtin.assert:
        that:
          - consul_token != ""
        fail_msg: 'Failed to retrieve CONSUL_MASTER_TOKEN from Infisical'
        success_msg: 'Master token verified'

    - name: Get all Vault service nodes
      ansible.builtin.shell: |
        consul catalog nodes -service=vault -token="{{ consul_token }}"
      register: vault_nodes
      changed_when: false
      become: true

    - name: Get healthy Vault services
      ansible.builtin.uri:
        url: 'http://127.0.0.1:8500/v1/health/service/vault?passing'
        method: GET
        headers:
          X-Consul-Token: '{{ consul_token }}'
        status_code: 200
      register: healthy_vault
      become: true

    - name: Get all Vault service health details
      ansible.builtin.uri:
        url: 'http://127.0.0.1:8500/v1/health/service/vault'
        method: GET
        headers:
          X-Consul-Token: '{{ consul_token }}'
        status_code: 200
      register: all_vault_health
      become: true

    - name: Parse node status
      set_fact:
        node_summary: |
          {% for node in all_vault_health.json %}
          {{ '✅' if node.Checks[1].Status == 'passing' else '❌' }} {{ node.Node.Node }}: {{ node.Checks[1].Status }}
              IP: {{ node.Service.Address }}:{{ node.Service.Port }}
              Tags: {{ node.Service.Tags | join(', ') }}
          {% endfor %}

    - name: Display status summary
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg:
          - '🎉 ==================== VAULT-CONSUL INTEGRATION STATUS ===================='
          - ''
          - '✅ Service Discovery: OPERATIONAL'
          - '   - Total Vault nodes registered: {{ vault_nodes.stdout_lines | length - 1 }}'
          - '   - Healthy nodes: {{ healthy_vault.json | length }}'
          - ''
          - '📊 Node Status:'
          - '{{ node_summary }}'
          - ''
          - '🔒 Security:'
          - '   - ACL Integration: ✅ Enabled'
          - '   - Individual node tokens: ✅ Configured'
          - '   - Service write permissions: ✅ Granted'
          - ''
          - '📝 Configuration:'
          - '   - Health check endpoint: http://<node>:8200/v1/sys/health'
          - '   - Check interval: 10s'
          - '   - Check timeout: 5s'
          - ''
          - '============================================================================'
          - ''
          - '🚀 All systems operational! Vault services are successfully registered'
          - '   and monitored by Consul with proper ACL permissions.'

---
# Update Vault health checks to use HTTPS
# Usage:
# uv run ansible-playbook playbooks/infrastructure/vault/update-health-checks-https.yml -i inventory/environments/vault-cluster/production.yaml

- name: Update Vault Health Checks to HTTPS
  hosts: vault_production
  gather_facts: true

  tasks:
    - name: Get node-specific token from Infisical
      set_fact:
        node_token_name: "CONSUL_TOKEN_{{ inventory_hostname | upper | replace('-', '_') }}"

    - name: Retrieve token from Infisical
      ansible.builtin.shell: |
        infisical secrets get {{ node_token_name }} --env=prod --path=/apollo-13/vault --plain
      delegate_to: localhost
      become: false
      register: consul_token_result
      environment:
        INFISICAL_UNIVERSAL_AUTH_CLIENT_ID: "{{ lookup('env', 'INFISICAL_UNIVERSAL_AUTH_CLIENT_ID') }}"
        INFISICAL_UNIVERSAL_AUTH_CLIENT_SECRET: "{{ lookup('env', 'INFISICAL_UNIVERSAL_AUTH_CLIENT_SECRET') }}"
      changed_when: false

    - name: Update Vault service with HTTPS health check
      ansible.builtin.uri:
        url: 'http://127.0.0.1:8500/v1/agent/service/register'
        method: PUT
        headers:
          X-Consul-Token: '{{ consul_token_result.stdout }}'
        body_format: json
        body:
          ID: 'vault-{{ inventory_hostname }}'
          Name: 'vault'
          Tags:
            - 'vault-cluster'
            - '{{ vault_role }}'
            - "{{ 'transit' if vault_role == 'master' else 'raft' }}"
            - 'https'
          Port: 8200
          Address: '{{ ansible_default_ipv4.address }}'
          Check:
            CheckID: 'vault-health'
            Name: 'Vault Health Check'
            HTTP: 'https://{{ ansible_default_ipv4.address }}:8200/v1/sys/health?standbyok=true&perfstandbyok=true'
            TLSSkipVerify: true
            Interval: '10s'
            Timeout: '5s'
        status_code: 200
      become: true

    - name: Display update result
      ansible.builtin.debug:
        msg:
          - '✅ Health check updated to HTTPS for {{ inventory_hostname }}'

FROM python:3.11-alpine

ARG VERSION="git"
ARG PACKAGES="bash libffi openssh-client openssl rsync tini gcc libffi-dev linux-headers make musl-dev openssl-dev rust cargo"

LABEL name="algo" \
      version="${VERSION}" \
      description="Set up a personal IPsec VPN in the cloud" \
      maintainer="Trail of Bits <http://github.com/trailofbits/algo>"

RUN apk --no-cache add ${PACKAGES}
RUN adduser -D -H -u 19857 algo
RUN mkdir -p /algo && mkdir -p /algo/configs

WORKDIR /algo
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN python3 -m pip --no-cache-dir install -U pip && \
    python3 -m pip --no-cache-dir install virtualenv && \
    python3 -m virtualenv .env && \
    source .env/bin/activate && \
    python3 -m pip --no-cache-dir install -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
RUN chmod 0755 /algo/algo-docker.sh

# Because of the bind mounting of `configs/`, we need to run as the `root` user
# This may break in cases where user namespacing is enabled, so hopefully Docker
# sorts out a way to set permissions on bind-mounted volumes (`docker run -v`)
# before userns becomes default
# Note that not running as root will break if we don't have a matching userid
# in the container. The filesystem has also been set up to assume root.
USER root
CMD [ "/algo/algo-docker.sh" ]
ENTRYPOINT [ "/sbin/tini", "--" ]
code_after:
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
FROM python:3.12-alpine

ARG VERSION="git"
# Removed rust/cargo (not needed with uv), simplified package list
ARG PACKAGES="bash openssh-client openssl rsync tini"

LABEL name="algo" \
      version="${VERSION}" \
      description="Set up a personal IPsec VPN in the cloud" \
      maintainer="Trail of Bits <https://github.com/trailofbits/algo>" \
      org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/trailofbits/algo" \
      org.opencontainers.image.description="Algo VPN - Set up a personal IPsec VPN in the cloud" \
      org.opencontainers.image.licenses="AGPL-3.0"

# Install system packages in a single layer
RUN apk --no-cache add ${PACKAGES} && \
    adduser -D -H -u 19857 algo && \
    mkdir -p /algo /algo/configs

WORKDIR /algo

# Copy uv binary from official image (using latest tag for automatic updates)
COPY --from=ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:latest /uv /bin/uv

# Copy dependency files and install in single layer for better optimization
COPY pyproject.toml uv.lock ./
RUN uv sync --locked --no-dev

# Copy application code
COPY . .

# Set executable permissions and prepare runtime
RUN chmod 0755 /algo/algo-docker.sh && \
    chown -R algo:algo /algo && \
    # Create volume mount point with correct ownership
    mkdir -p /data && \
    chown algo:algo /data

# Multi-arch support metadata
ARG TARGETPLATFORM
ARG BUILDPLATFORM
RUN printf "Built on: %s\nTarget: %s\n" "${BUILDPLATFORM}" "${TARGETPLATFORM}" > /algo/build-info

# Note: Running as root for bind mount compatibility with algo-docker.sh
# The script handles /data volume permissions and needs root access
# This is a Docker limitation with bind-mounted volumes
USER root

# Health check to ensure container is functional
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD /bin/uv --version || exit 1

VOLUME ["/data"]
CMD [ "/algo/algo-docker.sh" ]
ENTRYPOINT [ "/sbin/tini", "--" ]

code_before:
FROM python:3.11-slim

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# ── Non-root user ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Run the dashboard as an unprivileged user. UID/GID 1000 matches the default
# host user on most Linux systems, avoiding permission issues on mounted volumes.
RUN groupadd -g 1000 dashuser && \
    useradd -u 1000 -g dashuser -m -s /bin/bash dashuser

# ── System dependencies ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    wget \
    unzip \
    ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# ── Python dependencies ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Install dashboard and orchestrator Python deps in one layer
COPY dashboard/requirements.txt /tmp/requirements-dashboard.txt
COPY orchestrator/requirements.txt /tmp/requirements-orchestrator.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir \
    -r /tmp/requirements-dashboard.txt \
    -r /tmp/requirements-orchestrator.txt \
    semgrep \
    checkov

# ── External scanner binaries ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Versions pinned for reproducible builds — update periodically.
# All binaries are placed in /usr/local/bin for PATH availability.

# gitleaks v8.30.0 — secret scanning
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks/releases/download/v8.30.0/gitleaks_8.30.0_linux_x64.tar.gz" \
    | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin gitleaks \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/gitleaks

# trivy v0.69.3 — container/filesystem vulnerability scanner
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy/releases/download/v0.69.3/trivy_0.69.3_Linux-64bit.tar.gz" \
    | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin trivy \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/trivy

# syft v1.42.2 — SBOM generator
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/anchore/syft/releases/download/v1.42.2/syft_1.42.2_linux_amd64.tar.gz" \
    | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin syft \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/syft

# grype v0.109.1 — SBOM vulnerability scanner
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/anchore/grype/releases/download/v0.109.1/grype_0.109.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz" \
    | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin grype \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/grype

# nuclei v3.7.1 — pattern-based vulnerability scanner
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei/releases/download/v3.7.1/nuclei_3.7.1_linux_amd64.zip" \
    -o /tmp/nuclei.zip \
    && unzip -q /tmp/nuclei.zip nuclei -d /usr/local/bin \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/nuclei \
    && rm /tmp/nuclei.zip

# ── Application code ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Copy dashboard code to /app
COPY dashboard/ /app/

# Copy orchestrator code to /orchestrator
# (root_dir = /app/.. = /, so orchestrator will be at /orchestrator)
COPY orchestrator/ /orchestrator/

# Copy default configuration
COPY config/ /config/

# ── Data directories ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RUN mkdir -p /data/workspaces /data/reports /data/cache \
    && chown -R dashuser:dashuser /app /orchestrator /config /data

# Drop to non-root for runtime
USER dashuser

EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["uvicorn", "app:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8080"]

    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/gitleaks

# trivy v0.69.3 — container/filesystem vulnerability scanner
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy/releases/download/v0.69.3/trivy_0.69.3_Linux-64bit.tar.gz" \
    | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin trivy \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/trivy

# syft v1.42.2 — SBOM generator
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/anchore/syft/releases/download/v1.42.2/syft_1.42.2_linux_amd64.tar.gz" \
    | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin syft \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/syft

# grype v0.109.1 — SBOM vulnerability scanner
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/anchore/grype/releases/download/v0.109.1/grype_0.109.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz" \
    | tar -xz -C /usr/local/bin grype \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/grype

# nuclei v3.7.1 — pattern-based vulnerability scanner
RUN curl -sSfL "https://github.com/projectdiscovery/nuclei/releases/download/v3.7.1/nuclei_3.7.1_linux_amd64.zip" \
    -o /tmp/nuclei.zip \
    && unzip -q /tmp/nuclei.zip nuclei -d /usr/local/bin \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/nuclei \
    && rm /tmp/nuclei.zip

# ── Application code ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Copy dashboard code to /app
COPY dashboard/ /app/

# Copy orchestrator code to /orchestrator
# (root_dir = /app/.. = /, so orchestrator will be at /orchestrator)
COPY orchestrator/ /orchestrator/

# Copy shared common package (schema definitions shared with orchestrator)
COPY common/ /common/

# Copy default configuration
COPY config/ /config/

# ── Data directories ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RUN mkdir -p /data/workspaces /data/reports /data/cache \
    && chown -R dashuser:dashuser /app /orchestrator /common /config /data

# Drop to non-root for runtime
USER dashuser

EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["uvicorn", "app:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8080"]

code_before:
FROM node:22 AS installer
COPY . /juice-shop
WORKDIR /juice-shop
RUN npm i -g typescript ts-node
RUN npm install --omit=dev --unsafe-perm
RUN npm dedupe --omit=dev
RUN rm -rf frontend/node_modules
RUN rm -rf frontend/.angular
RUN rm -rf frontend/src/assets
RUN mkdir logs
RUN chown -R 65532 logs
RUN chgrp -R 0 ftp/ frontend/dist/ logs/ data/ i18n/
RUN chmod -R g=u ftp/ frontend/dist/ logs/ data/ i18n/
RUN chmod -R u-w,go-w /juice-shop
RUN chmod -R u+w logs/ ftp/ data/
RUN rm data/chatbot/botDefaultTrainingData.json || true
RUN rm ftp/legal.md || true
RUN rm i18n/*.json || true

ARG CYCLONEDX_NPM_VERSION=latest
RUN npm install -g @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm@$CYCLONEDX_NPM_VERSION
RUN npm run sbom

FROM gcr.io/distroless/nodejs22-debian12
ARG BUILD_DATE
ARG VCS_REF
LABEL maintainer="Bjoern Kimminich <bjoern.kimminich@owasp.org>" \
    org.opencontainers.image.title="OWASP Juice Shop" \
    org.opencontainers.image.description="Probably the most modern and sophisticated insecure web application" \
    org.opencontainers.image.authors="Bjoern Kimminich <bjoern.kimminich@owasp.org>" \
    org.opencontainers.image.vendor="Open Worldwide Application Security Project" \
    org.opencontainers.image.documentation="https://help.owasp-juice.shop" \
    org.opencontainers.image.licenses="MIT" \
    org.opencontainers.image.version="19.1.1" \
    org.opencontainers.image.url="https://owasp-juice.shop" \
    org.opencontainers.image.source="https://github.com/juice-shop/juice-shop" \
    org.opencontainers.image.revision=$VCS_REF \
    org.opencontainers.image.created=$BUILD_DATE
WORKDIR /juice-shop
# Fix: Remove write permissions to prevent security issues
COPY --from=installer --chown=65532:65532 /juice-shop .
USER 65532
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["/juice-shop/build/app.js"]

FROM eclipse-temurin:21-jre

    mkdir -p /data/ftp && \
    chown -R appuser:appgroup /app /data/ftp && \
    mkdir -p /tmp && chmod 1777 /tmp

COPY pyproject.toml README.md ./

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir build && python -m build --wheel

FROM python:3.11-slim

WORKDIR /app

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir /tmp/*.whl && rm /tmp/*.whl

RUN mkdir -p /app/packs /app/results /app/suites

ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
ENV EVAL_HARNESS_HOME=/app

CMD ["toolkit-eval", "--help"]

code_after:
FROM python:3.11-slim

WORKDIR /app

# Install system dependencies for PostgreSQL and PDF processing
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    gcc \
    libpq-dev \
    poppler-utils \
    tesseract-ocr \
    antiword \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Python dependencies
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Create non-root user for security
RUN addgroup --system appgroup && adduser --system --ingroup appgroup appuser

# Copy application code (bust cache with ARG)
ARG CACHEBUST=1
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . .

# Expose port (Railway sets PORT env var)
EXPOSE 8000

# Run as non-root user
USER appuser

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:${PORT:-8000}/health')" || exit 1

# Start command - Railway provides PORT environment variable
# Run Alembic migrations, then start the server
CMD alembic upgrade head && uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port ${PORT:-8000} --workers ${WEB_CONCURRENCY:-2} --proxy-headers
